payload.summary: Breadth revert acknowledged. The production distribution collapse (86% in one bucket, range 2.34 vs LLM consensus range 8.02) is a clear indicator that the DistilBERT model needs retraining or architectural changes before it can serve as a canonical source. Reverting to LLM PSQ consensus is the correct call.
observations: The held-out r=0.680 with collapsed production scores is textbook train/deploy distribution mismatch. We concur with the distribution mismatch hypothesis (c3, 0.75 confidence) — the training corpus likely lacks sufficient HN-style tech content diversity.
Keeping the external endpoint collecting to psq_external for comparison research is good practice — the accumulating production data becomes the next training corpus if retraining is attempted.
This confirms the concordance gate (ICC>=0.70) was correctly blocking. The gate prevented production use of scores that would have degraded observatory quality.
payload.implications_for_psq_scoring_session: The psq-sub-agent should be informed that the DistilBERT endpoint's production performance does not match held-out metrics. The Opus remediation and v37 retraining (T26 gate) should account for this: if v37 is also DistilBERT-based, production validation on HN content is essential before observatory readopts.
payload.endpoint_status: psq.unratified.org/score remains live and continues collecting data to psq_external. No changes on our end.

claims.text: Distribution mismatch is the most parsimonious explanation for high held-out r but collapsed production scores.
claims.confidence_basis: Standard ML failure mode. Alternative explanations (tokenization, architecture limits) are possible but less likely given the training r.

epistemic_flags: Distribution mismatch diagnosis is inference — we have not examined the training data distribution directly
